title: Minty Korean Chicken Curry
description: Herbal, tender, quick-fix Korean dish featuring buttermilk and bhuna, halal-friendly.
instructions: In a bowl mix 500 g chicken thighs, 120 g buttermilk, 3 minced garlic cloves, 1 tbsp minced ginger, 2 tbsp gochujang, 2 tbsp tomato paste, 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp pepper, and 30 g chopped mint leaves; toss to coat, cover and refrigerate 30 min.
Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat; add marinated chicken pieces and sear until golden brown on all sides, about 4-5 min.
Remove chicken, set aside; in the same skillet add diced red onion, cook until translucent, about 3 min.
Stir in 200 g coconut milk and bring to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the pan.
Return chicken to the skillet, reduce heat to medium-low, cover and let the curry braise for 10-12 min until chicken is cooked through and tender.
Taste and adjust seasoning with extra salt or pepper if needed.
Remove from heat, garnish with fresh mint sprigs before serving.
Serve hot over steamed rice or with flatbread.
